thread.title: Flags to fly at half-staff honoring deputies killed in Thursday crash
thread.title_full: Flags to fly at half-staff honoring deputies killed in Thursday crash

author: Grace Bellinghausen
title: Flags to fly at half-staff honoring deputies killed in Thursday crash
text:
PALM BEACH COUNTY, Fla. (CBS12) — To honor two deputies killed Thursday, Gov. Ron DeSantis ordered all U.S. flags and Florida flags fly half-staff throughout Palm Beach County on Dec. 3.
On Thursday, Nov. 21, Corporal Luis Paez and Deputy Sheriff Ralph Waller tragically died in a horrific car crash when they were struck by an SUV that went off the road after its driver swerved to avoid a car in front of her.
"These brave officers who made the ultimate sacrifice will always be remembered for their courage and service," said Gov. DeSantis.
See also: 'A shining light': Remembering fallen Palm Beach County deputies after traffic crash
According to DeSantis' news release, Corporal Paez began serving his community in 1988 as a Corrections Deputy before honorably serving as a Motor Deputy for more than 20 years, and Deputy Sheriff Waller served with the Royal Palm Beach Police Department before starting at the Palm Beach County Sheriff’s Office in 2008.
Flags will be flown at half-staff at all local and state buildings, installations, and grounds throughout Palm Beach County from 3 p.m. to sunset today, Friday, Nov. 22, and from sunrise to sunset on Tuesday, Dec. 3.
